workItem.title: Route dirty candidate failures into a bounded repair turn
workItem.body:

## Context

During game-api#80 finalization, a validation command legitimately synchronized uv.lock. CandidateHead correctly failed closed with candidate_workspace_dirty, but the generic retry resumed an implementation agent that repeatedly recorded evidence without resolving the dirty tracked file, causing the same failure again.

## Expected outcome

When a candidate durability check finds tracked changes, Symphony supplies the exact dirty-file diagnosis and prior command context to a bounded repair agent that must validate, commit or deliberately restore each change before the pipeline can resume.

## Scope

- Classify candidate_workspace_dirty as a repairable delivery failure
- Include exact tracked paths and generating command context in the repair prompt
- Require a clean tree and a new exact candidate HEAD before evidence or reviews continue
- Prevent generic retries from recording duplicate evidence without resolving the dirty tree
- Add lockfile regeneration and unrelated-change test cases

## Out of scope

- Automatically discard tracked changes
- Commit secrets or generated runtime files
- Relax the clean-tree durability gate

## Acceptance criteria

- A legitimate generated lockfile is validated, committed and published by a repair turn
- An unrelated or unsafe tracked change blocks with a precise diagnosis
- The same dirty-tree fingerprint cannot consume repeated generic retries
- Evidence and reviews restart only at the new remote-confirmed HEAD
- Complete Symphony tests and recovery gates pass
